name: Dot-Com Bubble and Aftermath
description: Internet enthusiasm, speculative valuations, and the correction that followed the late-1990s boom.

name: Pre-Financial-Crisis Expansion
description: Easy credit, rising asset prices, and broad economic growth before the 2007-2009 crisis.

name: Global Financial Crisis
description: The credit-market breakdown, recession, and severe equity-market decline of 2007-2009.

name: Post-Financial-Crisis Recovery
description: Rebuilding demand, low interest rates, and recovering risk appetite after the financial crisis.

name: Smartphone and Platform Era
description: Mobile computing and software platforms reshaped consumer behavior and technology markets.

name: Streaming Wars
description: Media distribution shifted online as incumbents and digital challengers competed for audiences.

name: Cloud Software Expansion
description: Businesses moved computing and software spending toward cloud-hosted and subscription services.

name: Pandemic Crash and Recovery
description: Markets fell sharply as activity stopped, then rebounded amid policy support and reopening expectations.

name: Pandemic Winners and Losers
description: Abrupt changes in work, mobility, and consumption created unusually divergent company outcomes.

name: Rate-Hike and Inflation Era
description: High inflation and rising interest rates challenged valuations, financing conditions, and consumer demand.
